item.title: Oracle Bonds Rally as Funding Plans Signal Borrowing Discipline
item.description: Oracle Corp.’s bonds rallied on Thursday even as its shares tumbled, after the database giant calmed debt investors by saying it doesn’t expect additional bond issuance this calendar year and outlining plans to raise $40 billion of debt and equity in the current fiscal year.

item.title: Novo’s Wegovy Pill Wins Approval in UK for Obesity Treatment
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S’s Wegovy pill was approved in the UK, opening up the obesity market to people who are wary of taking a shot.

item.title: OpenAI to Acquire Cloud Platform Ona to Support AI Agents
item.description: OpenAI has agreed to acquire Ona, a startup that offers cloud services to support artificial intelligence agents, part of a bid by the AI developer to make its technology more useful for businesses.

item.title: Your ETF of the Month: Software Isn’t Dead Yet
item.description: This fund proves that the industry’s demise at the hands of artificial intelligence has been somewhat exaggerated.

item.title: NTT Unit Said to Seek $1 Billion to Develop Data Centers in US
item.description: NTT Global Data Centers, a unit of Japan’s NTT Inc., is seeking at least $1 billion in fresh capital to fund development projects in the US, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: SEC Proposes to Scrap Trade-Through Rule for Equities Execution
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission proposed eliminating a rule that aims to ensure investors get better prices for their transactions.

item.title: Treasuries Rise on Lower Oil Prices, Mixed Inflation Readings
item.description: Treasuries rose as a drop in oil prices improved the outlook for US inflation.

item.title: SpaceX Treated as ‘Simply Too Risky’ for Funds With Governance Mandates
item.description: The list of sustainability-focused fund managers opting to blacklist SpaceX is growing, as they contemplate the unprecedented level of control that Elon Musk will hold over the rockets-to-chatbot behemoth.

item.title: Crusoe Pushed Aside at Wyoming AI Project After Google Concerns
item.description: On Tuesday, the data center developer Crusoe announced that it had “paused” a plan to build an AI campus in Wyoming that would draw enough electricity to power a city the size of Denver.

item.title: Europe Set to Get Rare Oman, UAE Oil as China Curbs Purchases
item.description: A wave of oil from the Middle East is set to arrive in Europe next month, a tangible example of how a collapse in Chinese imports is helping other countries to cope with the unprecedented supply disruption wrought by the Iran war.

item.title: Zambia’s Bond Buyback Hands More Impetus to 39% Local-Debt Rally
item.description: Zambia’s world-beating local debt may be poised for further gains after the government’s success buying back a potentially costly dollar bond.

item.title: SpaceX Valuation Is Cheap for Space Peers But Pricey as AI Stock
item.description: The jury is still out on whether SpaceX is primarily a rocket company, as its name suggests, or actually more of a telecom provider or artificial intelligence play. Its expected valuation doesn’t help resolve the confusion.

item.title: US Stocks Drift Higher as Chips Rise, Traders Assess Inflation
item.description: US stocks gained Thursday following two straight sessions of losses, led by strength in chipmakers as investors assessed developments in the Middle East and a report that showed US producer prices climbed at the fastest pace in more than three years.

item.title: Hormuz Oil Sneakouts Rise 50% as Iran and US Vie for Control
item.description: Oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz have surged by about 50% this month, according to Vortexa Ltd., underscoring what’s at stake as the US and Iran vie for control of the critical waterway.

item.title: Senegal, Morocco Are Africa’s Best World Cup Hopes, Model Shows
item.description: Senegal and Morocco are Africa’s best bets in the expanded FIFA 2026 World Cup, according to a model developed by Bloomberg Economics.

item.title: World Bank Says Two-Thirds of Economies Face Hit From Iran War
item.description: The World Bank cut its outlook for global growth this year and said two-thirds of economies have seen prospects deteriorate as the Middle East war disrupts commodity flows and raises the cost of imports.

item.title: Ghana’s World-Leading Stock Rally Spurs Liqueur Maker’s IPO
item.description: Ghana’s world-leading stock-market rally is encouraging companies to sell shares in initial public offerings and luring investors seeking higher returns with bond yields plunging.

item.title: Bessent Says US Will Extract Funds From Iran Accounts If Needed
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US will tap into frozen Iranian assets to pay for any damage the country inflicts on its allies in the Gulf.

item.title: Evercore Sees ‘Wild-Eyed’ FOMO Still Ahead as SpaceX Debut Looms
item.description: Stock investors are discounting the types of events that usually rattle Wall Street as fear of missing out on future gains grips the market on the eve of SpaceX’s trading debut, Evercore ISI’s Julian Emanuel said.

item.title: US Workers’ Health Insurance Costs Set to Rise, Survey Finds
item.description: Employers say higher prices are forcing them to raise premiums. Some will also stop covering GLP-1s.

item.title: DoorDash Built an AI Chatbot to Help With Orders, Reservations and Grocery Lists
item.description: DoorDash Inc. introduced an in-app artificial intelligence chatbot to help customers make restaurant and grocery orders, and suggested the technology could also be used to unlock new enterprise revenue streams.

item.title: Amazon Says Its Data Centers Use 2.5 Billion Gallons of Water
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. said its data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water worldwide last year, or about 5% of the amount metro Seattle consumes annually.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Draws More Than $70 Billion in Retail Orders
item.description: SpaceX’s initial public offering has attracted more than $70 billion in orders from retail investors, according to people familiar with the matter, as the potentially record-breaking debut enters the home stretch.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s ADIC Plans $15 Billion Leveraged Hedge Fund Bet
item.description: An Abu Dhabi-based investor is considering building exposure worth $15 billion with global hedge funds, people familiar with the matter said, an outlay that will mark one of the emirate’s most significant forays into the sector.

item.title: UK Energy Regulator Warns Data Centers Are Hoarding Grid Capacity
item.description: The UK’s energy regulator is concerned that data centers are reserving large amounts of grid capacity years before fully needed, further threatening the efficiency of networks facing long queues for new supply.

item.title: Odd Lots Is Taking Over Hong Kong (Or the Newsletter, At Least)
item.description: China markets, bowls of noodles, and more.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Jump to 229,000, Highest Since February
item.description: US initial jobless claims unexpectedly rose to the highest since February, potentially reflecting the usual volatility around school summer breaks and holidays.

item.title: US Producer Prices Rise at Fastest Pace Since November 2022
item.description: US producer prices rose in May at the fastest pace in more than three years as the fallout from the Iran war continued to fan inflation pressures.

item.title: Citigroup Offers Its First Investment-Grade Bonds of 2026
item.description: Citigroup Inc. is offering its first investment-grade bonds of 2026, following $123.3 billion of issuance by the five other biggest Wall Street banks this year.

item.title: ECB Hikes for First Time Since 2023 as Inflation Heats Up
item.description: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in almost three years, concluding it can no longer wait out the Iran war as inflation pressures intensify.
